full_text:
Did you know that setting up a voice server is WAY more complicated than a regular Minecraft mod? . You need a special networking setup! . And don’t forget to follow the steps carefully! .

You can host your server in TWO ways! . Use a Minecraft hosting provider, or go for self-hosting. . But remember, if you’re using a proxy, more setup is required! .

Change the voice chat port to make it your own! . For example, set 'port=24454' to use port 24454. . This simple tweak can change everything! .

Now, let’s test the voice chat! You can do this in-game with the command '/voicechat test <PLAYERNAME>'. . Or, go old school and test it from the command line! . But make sure the server is running first! .

Remember, general port checking tools won’t cut it for UDP ports! . You need to ensure everything is configured properly! . Get ready to dive into seamless communication in your games! .
